Chordata > Actinopterygii > Acanthuriformes > > Pomacanthidae

Yellowtail

Chaetodontoplus mesoleucus

Updated Sep 2020

Where seen?

Adults sometimes seen in fish traps. The adults are found solitary or in pairs, in silty reefs and sheltered lagoons.

Features

To about 18cm. Body oval with yellow tail, blackish, purplish brown rear portion and dorsal and anal fins, and a black bar on the head over the eyes. Large spine on the gill cover. Juveniles are bluish-black with white and blue narrow bars on the sides.

An adult caught in a fish trap. Kusu Island, Jun 04
Terumbu Pempang Tengah, Jul 12 Photo shared by Loh Kok Sheng on flickr .

What does it eat? It eats sponges, ascidians and filamentous algae.

Human uses: It is frequently sold in the aquarium trade.

Vermiculated angelfishes on Singapore shores

On wildsingapore flickr

  • Photos for free download
  • Map of sightings in Singapore

Links

  • Yellowtail or Vermiculated angelfish ( Chaetodontoplus mesoleucus ) Lim, Kelvin K. P. & Jeffrey K. Y. Low, 1998. A Guide to the Common Marine Fishes of Singapore . Singapore Science Centre. 163 pp.
  • Chaetodontoplus mesoleucus from the IUCN Red List.

References

  • Allen, Gerry, 2000. Marine Fishes of South-East Asia: A Field Guide for Anglers and Divers . Periplus Editions. 292 pp.
  • Lieske, Ewald and Robert Myers. 2001. Coral Reef Fishes of the World Periplus Editions. 400pp.
